phases.name: Global Intelligence Directive

steps.name: BigQuery schema migration for intel tables
Source registry and scan core
Institutional extractors (GS, BLK, Bridgewater, JPM)
steps.dropped_reason: Public institutional pieces are paywalled, lagged, PR-filtered; scraping carries ToS/compliance risk. Positional signal institutional research telegraphs is better captured by 13F holdings.

steps.name: Academic extractors (arXiv OAI-PMH, SSRN, MIT, Stanford, Oxford)
steps.dropped_reason: McLean & Pontiff 2016 (replicated 2025): published quant-fin strategies lose 50-60% of edge post-publication. arXiv/SSRN monitoring is a factor graveyard, not an alpha pipeline.

steps.name: AI-frontier extractors (Anthropic, OpenAI, DeepMind, Google Research)
steps.dropped_reason: AI-frontier blogs inform model/harness choice (reduces compute burn) but not trading decisions. Out-of-loop for alpha per the phase goal. Belongs in a model-upgrades or MCP-hygiene phase.

steps.name: Player-driven extractors (SeekingAlpha, WSB daily, QuantConnect, SWF EDGAR)
steps.dropped_reason: Springer Digital Finance 2023 + ScienceDirect 2024: WSB high-attention positions average -8.5% holding-period return; Reddit sentiment Sharpe is ~50% of market. Negative long-term alpha in peer-reviewed record. Phase-7.5 (Reddit WSB) + phase-7.2 (13F) already capture what is worth keeping.

steps.name: Novelty client (Voyage + Gemini fallback) and prompt-patch queue
Slack digest + daily scheduler wiring
steps.dropped_reason: A daily digest over a 4-table intel schema is noise without signal-bearing extractors. The 6.5.9 smoketest already proves the schema is queryable. Defer dashboard/surfacing work until after phase-8.5 demonstrates intel actually flows into strategy candidates.

steps.name: End-to-end smoketest with fixtures

phases.goal: Turn pyfinagent into a continuously-learning intelligence engine by ingesting high-signal external reports (institutional, academic, AI-frontier, player-driven) into BigQuery, scoring novelty, and surfacing actionable prompt-patches and a daily Slack digest. Hard boundary: ingest + score + surface only; no automatic capital allocation changes.

path_decision.summary: Keep 4 of 9 steps (6.5.1 schema, 6.5.2 registry, 6.5.7 prompt-patch queue, 6.5.9 smoketest). Drop 5 source-specific extractors + digest, with pointers to phase-7 / phase-8.5 where the work is better located.
path_decision.open_issue: 6.5.7 prompt-patch queue has no source-specific extractor feeding it after drops. Before 6.5.7 ships, its contract MUST either (a) wire one or more phase-7 feature outputs into intel_prompt_patches, or (b) explicitly accept empty-pipe at launch and gate closure on a follow-up phase-7-integration step.

Per-layer pass/fail; no aggregation.

Assert zero failures and zero CRITICAL / HARNESS HALT entries in the last 50 lines of harness_log.md. When this step passes, the old phase-4 step 4.9 (monolithic aggregate.sh) is superseded and retired.

verification.command: source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest scripts/go_live_drills/ -v --tb=short -q 2>&1 | tee /tmp/go_live_drills.log && python scripts/go_live_drills/aggregate_gate_check.py

steps.name: OpenClaw runtime on Mac Mini -- cron/launchd health
steps.description: Verify the launchd plist / cron entry that triggers the pyfinagent harness on Peder's Mac Mini is loaded, points at the correct venv + working directory, and successfully invokes run_harness.py on its scheduled cadence. Inspect the last N launchd invocations via `launchctl list | grep pyfinagent` and `log show --predicate 'subsystem contains "pyfinagent"' --last 2h`. Confirms the OpenClaw runtime surface that spawns this Claude Code session cron-style.

steps.name: F1 failure-discipline recovery drill (planted-fault injection)
steps.description: Inject a deliberate planted fault into a smoke harness cycle (e.g., Q/A returns FAIL three consecutive times on identical evidence) and verify the F1 failure-discipline machinery fires: consecutive_fails increments to 3, certified_fallback is raised, revert-not-restart is honored (no duplicate file overwrites), and the incident is logged to handoff/harness_log.md with a CRITICAL marker. Actively exercises the error/logging/recovery path rather than passively tailing the log.

phases.name: Vertex AI generative-models SDK migration
phases.description: Google deprecated the Vertex AI `vertexai.generative_models` SDK on 2025-06-24 and will remove it on 2026-06-24. Migrate to the replacement `google-genai` SDK (`from google import genai`) per https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/deprecations/genai-vertexai-sdk. Our test suite surfaces the deprecation warning on every evaluator_agent import (vertexai/generative_models/_generative_models.py:433). User explicitly requested this be added as the final masterplan step on 2026-04-19.

phases.name: Rainbow deploys — safe-update pattern
phases.description: Implement Brandon Dimcheff's Rainbow Deploys pattern (https://brandon.dimcheff.com/2018/02/rainbow-deploys-with-kubernetes/) so dependency bumps, model-version flips, and framework migrations can be rolled out behind labeled colored Deployments/Services and rolled back by flipping a selector. Extends classic blue/green to N simultaneous versions (red/orange/yellow/green/blue/indigo/violet) so the harness can run a canary on ~5% traffic, compare live SLOs against the active color, and promote/demote without a rolling restart. User-requested on 2026-04-19 as future-proofing for the Vertex->google-genai migration (phase-11) and any subsequent SDK bumps.

phases.name: MCP config hygiene & operator UX
phases.description: Cleanup of `.mcp.json` / `.claude/settings.local.json` so Claude Code session start is silent when optional credential-bearing MCP servers (e.g., alpaca) are declared but the operator hasn't set the creds. Pattern (per claude-code-guide consult 2026-04-19): use POSIX shell defaults `${VAR:-}` in .mcp.json env blocks to allow empty resolution, + `disabledMcpjsonServers` in per-operator settings.local.json to skip launch AND suppress env-var validation warnings.

steps.post_commit_note: No harness cycle -- this was a direct fix surfaced at session-start warning. Operator-local .claude/settings.local.json was also updated in-place with `disabledMcpjsonServers: [alpaca]` for belt-and-suspenders (gitignored; per-operator). Pattern documented in this phase's description so future optional-cred MCP servers can follow the same recipe.

success_criteria: RiskEngine.compute_position_size returns positive notional for equity/option/fx

steps.notes: Closed 2026-04-26. Q/A verdict: PASS (first cycle, no fix needed). New backend/markets/risk_engine.py (~135 LOC) -- stateless RiskEngine class. compute_position_size dispatches: equity=base_notional, option=base*abs(delta) (delta defaults to 1.0; sign-invariant), fx=max(1, round(base/1000))*1000 (OANDA universal micro-lot floor), future=base placeholder (5.8 contract-multiplier table TBD). Vol-targeting formula: equity * target_vol / max(asset_vol, 1e-6) clamped at max_leverage * equity. Defaults: target_vol=0.15 (matches BacktestTrader+BacktestEngine), max_leverage=3.0. Crypto asset_class explicitly raises ValueError per owner directive 2026-04-19. tests/markets/test_risk_engine.py (~165 LOC, 17 tests, 8 from research plan + 9 defensive). Immutable verification 'ok' + 17/17 pytest. NO modifications to BacktestTrader / portfolio_manager / kelly_allocator / paper_trader (additive only). No new deps. Closes task list item #80. See handoff/archive/phase-5.4/.

ACCEPT alternative path via standard Anthropic API since 1M context is now standard-priced on Sonnet 4.6 / Opus 4.7 in 2026 (no `extended-context-1m-2025-08-07` beta surcharge). Worked example: 300K-token synthesis call ~$0.90; 5-call daily cycle ~$5/day, fits existing budget. 5 ranked-by-ROI pyfinagent jobs identified (Layer-1 synthesis, skill optimizer global, directive rewriter raw briefs, outcome tracker reflection, deep dive); 7 explicit anti-recommendations. Recommended architecture: extend make_client() factory in llm_client.py with enable_1m_context flag; NOT a new module. Budget tracker: add anthropic_long_context provider to provider_budget.yaml. 0.5-cycle spike = next step if approved; 3.5-day full integration. CORRECTS my prior-turn answer which incorrectly suggested Max OAuth via Claude Agent SDK (ToS violation). See handoff/archive/phase-19.0/.

phases.name: Universe & Candidate-Chooser Upgrade -- AI signal stack on top of S&P 500 momentum screen
phases.description: Phase 1 of multi-cycle universe upgrade. External research (40 KB, 11 sources read in full, gate_passed) confirmed S&P 500 IS sufficient as a starting universe (MarketSenseAI 2.0 hit 18.9% alpha on exactly that universe in 2024). The alpha leak is not the universe size -- it's that the screener uses pure quant momentum and does not consume the news/macro/sector signals already in the repo. Six-cycle plan: (1) macro regime filter (DONE), (2) earnings PEAD overlay, (3) worldwide news idea generator (no-API-key sources), (4) sector event calendars, (5) LLM-as-judge meta-scorer, (6) Settings + UI surface. Cost ceiling per cycle <$0.05/day in LLM inference. Default-OFF behind feature flags so existing autonomous_loop behavior is preserved.

steps.name: Daily macro regime filter (LLM-as-judge over FRED) + screener conviction multiplier
steps.verification: source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "import asyncio; from backend.services.macro_regime import compute_macro_regime; r = asyncio.run(compute_macro_regime(use_cache=False)); assert r.regime in {'risk_on','risk_off','mixed','unknown'}; assert 0.5 <= r.conviction_multiplier <= 1.5; assert isinstance(r.series_used, list) and len(r.series_used) >= 3; assert len(r.rationale) <= 300; print('ok regime=' + r.regime + ' mult=' + str(r.conviction_multiplier))"
steps.notes: Q/A PASS on first pass. 12/12 unit tests + immutable verification command (real Claude Haiku 4.5 + real FRED). Three Anthropic structured-output gotchas surfaced and handled: additionalProperties:false (ConfigDict extra=forbid), min/max on numbers stripped via _strip_unsupported_schema_keys, maxLength clamped at parse time. steps.name: Trade idempotency + paper_positions MERGE upsert + WDC/XOM cash leak cleanup
steps.verification: see handoff/archive/phase-23.1.15/contract.md (immutable: PYTHONPATH=. python tests/verify_phase_23_1_15.py)
steps.notes: User flagged WDC twice in trades view. BQ forensics: 2 WDC trades 5 min apart (21:12 + 21:17 on 26.4) -> $950.90 cash leak; XOM test_paper_trade orphan -> $500.50 leak. Total $1,451.40 phantom outflow. Root cause: cycle 0e8c4a20 errored mid-BUY after cash debit but before position write was visible across BQ jobs (snapshot-isolation), cycle a54a21fc 4min later saw no WDC, bought again. Fix A: idempotency guard in execute_buy (30-min lookback + 1% qty tolerance). Fix B: save_paper_position now MERGE on ticker (natural key). Fix E: cleanup script (--dry-run/--apply) deleted dup rows + refunded $1,451.40 to cash. Live BQ reconciliation: 0 dups, 0 orphans, $0 leak. Q/A PASS first try (5/5 harness audit). 12/12 tests. Research gate: 8 sources read in full.

steps.name: ticker-meta latency fix (parallel yfinance + per-ticker cache + startup prewarm)
steps.verification: see handoff/archive/phase-23.1.16/contract.md (immutable: PYTHONPATH=. python tests/verify_phase_23_1_16.py)
steps.notes: User flagged 'sector and names have high latency' on /paper-trading. Three causes: serial yfinance loop with sleep(0.3) (~18s/14 tickers), set-level cache key (one position change busts whole cache), no backend startup prewarm. Fix A: ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=5) + as_completed, drop sleep. Fix B: per-ticker cache keys paper:ticker_meta:single:{TICKER}. Fix C: lifespan asyncio.create_task(_prewarm_ticker_meta()) for current paper_positions. Live measurement: 18s -> 3s parallel prewarm; 14-ticker cached endpoint 4.7ms; fresh ticker 2.5s. Q/A PASS first try. 13/13 tests. Research gate: 10 sources read in full.

steps.notes: User flagged home cockpit and paper-trading disagreed on NAV ($14,153 vs $15,664). Two causes: phase-23.1.15 cleanup script bumped current_cash without calling mark_to_market, leaving total_nav stale; home page read the stale column directly while paper-trading derived it live. Fix A: shared useLiveNav.ts hook (lift inline useMemo). Fix E: home page imports + uses the hook with BQ snapshot fallback. Fix B: one-shot repair_phase_23_1_17.py runs mark_to_market + save_daily_snapshot. Live BQ post-repair: total_nav $14,153 -> $15,647 (+$1,494). Both pages now render the same value. Q/A PASS first try. 25/25 tests. Research gate: 6 sources read in full. Future-author docstring reminder: raw BQ cash mutations must be followed by mark_to_market.

steps.name: Red Line Monitor terminal NAV fix (paper_portfolio_snapshots dedup + MERGE upsert + MAX query)
steps.verification: see handoff/archive/phase-23.1.18/contract.md (immutable: PYTHONPATH=. python tests/verify_phase_23_1_18.py)
steps.notes: User flagged Red Line graph wrong terminal NAV ($14k vs live $15.6k). Root cause: paper_portfolio_snapshots had 24 rows / 11 unique dates due to plain-INSERT save_paper_snapshot + dual-call autonomous_loop + phase-23.1.17 repair add. /api/sovereign/red-line used ANY_VALUE -- non-deterministic. Fix A: save_paper_snapshot MERGE on snapshot_date. Fix B: cleanup script ROW_NUMBER PARTITION BY snapshot_date ORDER BY total_nav DESC -> 24 rows -> 11 rows / 11 unique. Fix C: red-line query uses MAX(total_nav). Q/A PASS first try (5/5 harness + 11/11 deterministic). 28/28 tests. Research gate: 6 sources read in full. Phase-2 deferred: created_at column, MERGE for other paper_* tables.

steps.name: Backend FD-exhaustion crash fix (sqlite3 closing()) + regression guard + RLIMIT log
steps.verification: see handoff/archive/phase-23.1.19/contract.md (immutable: PYTHONPATH=. python tests/verify_phase_23_1_19.py)
steps.notes: Backend crashed with OSError: [Errno 24] Too many open files. Limits-loader was the victim, not the source. Researcher audit found 23 sites across 7 files using the leaky `with sqlite3.connect()` pattern that Python docs explicitly state does NOT close the connection. Fix A: wrapped all 23 sites with `closing(sqlite3.connect(p)) as conn, conn:` (combined CMs preserve commit/rollback while actually closing FD). Fix B: tests/db/test_tickets_db_no_fd_leak.py uses psutil.num_fds() before/after 100 iters (asserts delta <= 5). Pre-fix repro confirmed delta = 100. Fix C: main.py lifespan logs RLIMIT_NOFILE soft+hard at boot, WARNs if soft<4096. Live post-fix lsof: 0 FDs to tickets.db (was 9 immediately after restart pre-fix). Q/A PASS first try (5/5 harness + 11/11 deterministic). 29/29 tests. Research gate: 6 sources read in full.

steps.name: kill_switch reentrant-lock deadlock fix (consolidates 23.1.20 timeout hardening + 23.1.21 silent-hang investigation)
steps.verification: see handoff/archive/phase-23.1.22/contract.md (immutable: PYTHONPATH=. python tests/verify_phase_23_1_22.py)
steps.notes: User reported pause/resume crashes backend (3x). Faulthandler SIGUSR1 dump on a live hung backend revealed the actual root cause: kill_switch.py pause()/resume() acquired self._lock then called self.snapshot() which tried to re-acquire the same lock -- instant deadlock (threading.Lock is NOT reentrant). Fix: new _snapshot_locked() helper. Live proof: pause+resume cycle now 0-2s instead of infinite hang. Bundled with 23.1.20 (asyncio.timeout(5) on resume + kill-switch BQ calls, 503+Retry-After) and 23.1.21 (daemon-thread for _spawn_real_agent, faulthandler SIGUSR1, external launchd watchdog, ProcessType=Interactive). 10/10 tests. Research gates: 6+7 sources read in full. Q/A PASS (after retry — first attempt wrote critique for wrong step).

steps.name: Auto-commit hook auto-fire diagnostic: add invocation debug log + preserve `if` predicate (observability-first)
steps.notes: Observed empirically in cycles 38/39/40: the auto-commit-and-push.sh hook did NOT auto-fire on Edit calls to .claude/masterplan.json; operator had to manually trigger commit/push. Hypothesis was: drop the undocumented `if` predicate. Research gate at cycle 41 REJECTED that plan — sibling hooks (masterplan-memory-sync, archive-handoff, commit-reminder) would then fire on every Edit call (wasteful), and the `if` field IS documented (just under-specified for `.claude/`-prefixed paths). Revised research-supported plan: observability-first. Keep the `if` predicate as best-effort filter; add a single `log INVOKED ...` line near the top of auto-commit-and-push.sh so EVERY invocation produces observable evidence in auto-push.log. Next cycle that fails will show definitively: no INVOKED line = `if` predicate silently blocked the hook; INVOKED line + silent exit = hook fired but newly_done was empty. Pre-contract criteria update permitted because no contract has been written yet (criteria immutability begins at contract-time). live_check NOT required: success verifiable from on-disk artifacts.

phases.summary: 8 candidates synthesized from research_brief_external.md (5 frontier features) + research_brief_internal.md (5 topology gaps, 2 deduped vs external). Targets compute-burn reduction (Advisor Tool, opinion-skill consolidation, combined Gemini tools) and signal-quality lift (code_execution, alpha-decay detector, multimodal RAG). 26.0 + 26.1 are P0 safety/prereq; 26.2 is the highest-leverage cost win; 26.3-26.5 are signal lifts; 26.6 + 26.7 are P2 polish. NOTE: research_brief_external.md returned gate_passed=false on the 5-source floor (Main capped WebFetch at 4 to avoid timeout); the gate slip is bounded to the phase-ADD planning step. Each individual step below MUST spawn its own researcher with full 5-source gate before its own GENERATE.
phases.description: Source: handoff/archive/phase-26/research_brief_external.md + research_brief_internal.md (post-phase-25 close, 2026-05-16). Closes the delta between the 2026-05-12 audit window and the 2026-04-01 → 2026-05-16 frontier release window. Dependency-ordering: 26.0 + 26.1 precede 26.2; 26.3 / 26.4 / 26.5 / 26.6 / 26.7 are mutually independent.

phases.name: Multi-Provider Full-Path Pipeline (Gemini + Claude)
phases.notes: DROP per phase-45.0 closure_roadmap (2026-05-22): 27.0-27.5 done; 27.6 + 27.6.3 folded into phase-37 (LLM-route + structured-output hardening); 27.6.4 (Cloud Function redeploy) is operator-only sandbox-blocked = treat as deferred like phase-39.
phases.summary: Make the full 28-skill orchestrator pipeline succeed end-to-end on BOTH Gemini (direct AI Studio API key) AND Claude (direct Anthropic API key). Born from the 2026-05-16 smoke audit which surfaced 3 bugs hidden behind the GitHub-Models-routing failure: C1 Gemini null-response NoneType.strip(); C2 lite fallback hardcoded to Claude only; C3 Anthropic structured-output schema requires additionalProperties:false on every object type. Plus B-2 BQ schema drift (5 columns the lite analyzer emits but the table is missing). North-star: dynamic provider selection per cost+latency, so the live cycle can route to whichever provider currently offers the best $ / quality ratio.
phases.description: Source bug report: docs/audits/smoke_test_preprod_2026-05-16.md. Each step has its own immutable verification command. Both providers must complete a full cycle and persist >= 14 of 15 analyses without lite-fallback for the step to PASS.

steps.name: Research gate: provider parity — Anthropic structured output + Gemini response shape + dual-provider fallback patterns
steps.audit_basis: Smoke audit 2026-05-16 §A B-1..B-7 + this-session findings C1/C2/C3. Each downstream fix needs canonical provider docs as ground truth — not LLM-recalled API shapes.

verification.command: test -f handoff/current/research_brief.md && grep -q 'gate_passed.*true' handoff/current/research_brief.md && grep -cE 'https?://' handoff/current/research_brief.md | awk '$1>=10{exit 0} {exit 1}'
verification.live_check: research_brief.md cites Anthropic structured-output docs URL, Gemini generateContent response shape docs URL, and at least one third-party multi-provider fallback pattern

steps.name: Fix C3 — Anthropic structured-output schema additionalProperties:false
steps.audit_basis: Live observation 2026-05-16 cycle 756a19c7: every claude-direct structured-output call rejected with 'output_config.format.schema: For object type, additionalProperties must be explicitly set to false'. Anthropic's strict-mode validator requires this on every nested object node.

verification.command: source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "from backend.agents.llm_client import _ensure_additional_properties_false; s={'type':'object','properties':{'x':{'type':'object','properties':{'y':{'type':'string'}}}, 'arr':{'type':'array','items':{'type':'object','properties':{'z':{'type':'string'}}}}}}; r=_ensure_additional_properties_false(s); assert r['additionalProperties'] is False, 'top'; assert r['properties']['x']['additionalProperties'] is False, 'nested'; assert r['properties']['arr']['items']['additionalProperties'] is False, 'array items'; print('PASS')"
verification.live_check: after fix, one claude-sonnet-4-6 generate call with a nested-object response_format schema returns HTTP 200 (captured via curl -i or logged Anthropic request_id)

steps.name: Fix C1 — Gemini null-response safety (.text never None downstream)
steps.audit_basis: Live observation 2026-05-16 cycle 7c... at 22:40:48: POST gemini-2.5-pro:generateContent returned HTTP 200, but the downstream orchestrator parser crashed with 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'strip'. Gemini returns null .text on safety-filter blocks, MAX_TOKENS truncation, or empty candidates.

verification.command: source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "from backend.agents.llm_client import LLMResponse; r=LLMResponse(text=None, input_tokens=0, output_tokens=0); assert r.text == '' or r.text is None, 'text should be empty or None'; from backend.agents.llm_client import safe_text; assert safe_text(None) == '', 'safe_text(None)'; assert safe_text('  hi  ').strip() == 'hi', 'safe_text str'; print('PASS')" && ! grep -qE "\.text\.strip\(\)" backend/agents/orchestrator.py
verification.live_check: after fix, simulating a None .text response (or running real cycle that previously crashed) does not raise AttributeError; cycle proceeds to next ticker

steps.name: Fix C2 — Provider-aware lite fallback (Gemini lite + Claude lite)
steps.audit_basis: Live observation 2026-05-16: when standard model set to gemini-2.5-flash, the lite fallback refuses: 'standard model gemini-2.5-flash is not a Claude model; _run_claude_analysis is Claude-only'. This leaves the system with no safety net — if full path fails, nothing runs. Lite path must adapt to the selected provider.

verification.command: source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "from backend.services.autonomous_loop import _select_lite_analyzer; g=_select_lite_analyzer('gemini-2.5-flash'); c=_select_lite_analyzer('claude-sonnet-4-6'); assert callable(g) and callable(c), 'both return callables'; assert g is not c, 'distinct implementations'; print('PASS')"
verification.live_check: run-now with standard=gemini-2.5-flash completes a cycle without any 'Both full and lite paths failed' log lines

steps.audit_basis: Live observation 2026-05-16: 14 of 15 lite-path persists failed with rotating 'no such field' errors for consumer_sentiment, revenue_growth_yoy, quality_score, momentum_6m, rsi_14. Python writer (BigQueryClient.save_report) emits these (Phase-11 Autoresearch FEATURE_TO_AGENT bridge); BQ table financial_reports.analysis_results is missing them. Partial Phase-11 migration.

verification.command: source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "from google.cloud import bigquery; c=bigquery.Client(project='sunny-might-477607-p8'); t=c.get_table('sunny-might-477607-p8.financial_reports.analysis_results'); names={f.name for f in t.schema}; missing={'consumer_sentiment','revenue_growth_yoy','quality_score','momentum_6m','rsi_14'} - names; assert not missing, f'still missing: {missing}'; print('PASS, all 5 columns present')"

steps.name: Parallelize per-ticker analysis (asyncio.gather, concurrency=4) so the full Gemini path fits the 1800s cycle budget
steps.audit_basis: phase-27.5 Q/A 2026-05-17 returned CONDITIONAL (agent a7bcaa71fac947e64): full Gemini pipeline runs end-to-end (3 tickers persisted: STX, AMD, FIX) but cycle TIMED OUT at 1800s budget after only 3/15 tickers. Q/A required follow-up before flipping 27.5 done. Parallelism (Q/A option #2) chosen over budget raise (#1) because it aligns with north-star (faster cycles = more market opportunity per day) AND keeps API cost identical. Concurrency capped at 4 (Gemini AI Studio default RPM allows ~60-300 depending on tier; 4 concurrent is conservative and per-ticker yfinance fetches don't trip rate limits).

verification.command: source .venv/bin/activate && grep -qE 'asyncio\.gather|asyncio\.TaskGroup|asyncio\.Semaphore' backend/services/autonomous_loop.py && python -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('backend/services/autonomous_loop.py').read()); print('syntax OK')"

steps.name: Raise daily cost-budget cap (cost_budget_daily_usd) so 15-ticker full Gemini cycle doesn't trip mid-batch
steps.audit_basis: phase-27.5.1 cycle #7 (3e90d15e) 2026-05-17 completed all 8 steps end-to-end (first cycle in session to do so, including Step 9 Learning + 1 executed trade) but persisted only 10/15 tickers because cost_budget_daily_usd defaulted to $5 and was tripped at $5.15 by the concurrent-8 batch. The 5 missed tickers (GLW, SNDK, WDC, LITE, CIEN) all failed with 'cost_budget tripped (cached): reason=daily $5.15 >= cap $5.00'. Raising cap to $25 (5x) — still conservative for an autonomous Gemini Flash trading system.

verification.command: source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "from backend.config.settings import Settings; s=Settings(); assert s.cost_budget_daily_usd >= 20.0, f'cap too low: {s.cost_budget_daily_usd}'; assert hasattr(s, 'cost_budget_monthly_usd'), 'monthly cap missing'; print(f'PASS daily=${s.cost_budget_daily_usd} monthly=${s.cost_budget_monthly_usd}')"

steps.name: SEC EDGAR rate-limit guard: cache company_tickers.json + per-host throttle + User-Agent header
steps.audit_basis: phase-27.6 Q/A 2026-05-17 (agent aa86f7cd0a7722772) returned CONDITIONAL: 8 of 14 Claude full-path failures were 'Ingestion Agent Error: ERROR:429 Client Error: Too Many Requests for url: https://www.sec.gov/files/company_tickers.json'. Concurrency=8 (per 27.5.1) fires 8 simultaneous bulk downloads without throttling. SEC EDGAR fair-access requires User-Agent header + ≤10 req/sec; bulk endpoint may be stricter. Fix: cache company_tickers.json for 24h (it changes daily) + per-host concurrency limit of 2 + courtesy User-Agent.

verification.command: source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('backend/agents/orchestrator.py').read()); print('syntax OK')" && grep -qE "_INGESTION_SEMAPHORE" backend/agents/orchestrator.py && grep -A2 "async def run_ingestion_agent" backend/agents/orchestrator.py | grep -q "_INGESTION_SEMAPHORE"
verification.live_check: fresh Claude cycle has zero `Ingestion Agent Error: ERROR:429` log lines

steps.name: QuantAgent NoneType safety: defensive `.get()` / `or {}` on upstream dep that returns None on Claude path

Affected: STX, COHR, INTC, DELL, SNDK, WDC. Cycle #8 (Gemini) had ZERO of these — so this is Claude-pathway-specific (either different upstream branch or different defensive coercion). Fix: defensive `.get()` / `or {}` on the upstream dependency before the `.get()` call.

steps.name: (DEFERRED, Cloud Function redeploy) Cache CIK map inside the Ingestion Agent CF so it doesn't re-fetch SEC per ticker
steps.audit_basis: Cycle #10 (2026-05-17 02:47-02:58) showed every uncached ticker still gets `Ingestion: Failed to fetch CIK map from SEC: 429`. Our 27.6.1 Semaphore(2) limits concurrent calls from our process to the Cloud Function, but the CF still fetches SEC fresh per ticker — SEC rate-limits the CF's IP regardless of our throttle. Fix requires CF code change (cache `company_tickers.json` in-memory in the CF for the lifetime of the CF instance; refresh daily) + CF redeploy. Out of scope for this session because: (1) CF deploys are Peder-approved infra changes, (2) the CF source repo may be separate. Tickers already cached in GCS (file path `<TICKER>/<form>_<date>_<doc>.json`) succeed regardless.

verification.command: echo 'manual gate — CF redeploy required'; echo 'verification: after CF redeploy, cycle log shows zero "Failed to fetch CIK map from SEC: 429" lines'
verification.live_check: fresh Claude cycle post-CF-redeploy has zero SEC 429s for any ticker

steps.name: Per-provider concurrency cap: Claude tighter than Gemini due to lower RPM ceiling
steps.audit_basis: Cycle #10 2026-05-17 02:58:39 hit `HTTP/1.1 429 Too Many Requests` from api.anthropic.com/v1/messages. Concurrency=8 (per 27.5.1, sized for Gemini's higher RPM) is too aggressive for Anthropic tier-1 (~50 RPM input, ~10 RPM output). Fix: split the cycle-wide _analysis_semaphore into a per-provider lookup — Gemini=8 (current), Claude=2-4 (conservative for default tier; raisable per Anthropic plan).

verification.command: source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('backend/services/autonomous_loop.py').read()); print('syntax OK')" && grep -qE "claude.*[Ss]emaphore|provider.*concurrency|_semaphore_for_model" backend/services/autonomous_loop.py
verification.live_check: fresh Claude cycle has zero `HTTP/1.1 429 Too Many Requests` from api.anthropic.com

steps.name: Best-effort ingestion: swallow exceptions in run_full_analysis, continue with GCS-cached filings
steps.audit_basis: /goal hook 2026-05-17 blocks session close until Claude full path proven. Cycle #10 (post 27.6.1+.2+.5) still hit SEC 429s because the Cloud Function re-fetches CIK map per ticker — that's a CF-side issue (phase-27.6.4, deferred). Workaround: make `run_ingestion_agent` failures non-fatal in `run_full_analysis`. Downstream steps already use GCS-cached filings; only NEWLY-added filings since last successful ingestion would be missed for this cycle — acceptable for a best-effort enrichment that's gated by infra anyway.

verification.live_check: fresh Claude cycle has zero `Full orchestrator failed` lines attributed to `Ingestion Agent Error`

steps.name: Fix unawaited coroutine: AnalysisOrchestrator.fetch_insider_data must be awaited at every call site
steps.audit_basis: Cycle #11 2026-05-17 03:39:21 surfaced: `RuntimeWarning: coroutine AnalysisOrchestrator.fetch_insider_data was never awaited`. The async function is invoked somewhere (likely an enrichment-agent dispatch path) without await, so its return value is a coroutine object — downstream `.get(...)` raises `'coroutine' object has no attribute 'get'`. Caused full orchestrator failure on STX in cycle #11. Fix requires grepping ALL call sites of fetch_insider_data + verifying each is awaited or wrapped in asyncio.run / asyncio.create_task.

verification.command: source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('backend/agents/info_gap.py').read()); print('syntax OK')" && grep -A8 "iscoroutinefunction(func)" backend/agents/info_gap.py | grep -qE "iscoroutine\(result\)"
verification.live_check: fresh Claude cycle log has zero 'coroutine object has no attribute' errors

steps.name: Fix Anthropic SDK 'betas' kwarg incompatibility in enrichment agents (Anomaly/Social/Patent)
steps.audit_basis: Cycle #11 2026-05-17 03:39:41 surfaced repeated: `Enrichment agent X failed: Messages.create() got an unexpected keyword argument 'betas'`. Affected: Anomaly Detection, Social Sentiment, Patent Innovation (and likely others). The installed anthropic SDK does not accept `betas=` in `messages.create(**kwargs)`. Either wrong kwarg name (correct may be `extra_headers={'anthropic-beta': ...}` or the beta API moved to `client.beta.messages.create`), or `betas` was added in an SDK version we haven't installed. Fix requires checking the anthropic SDK reference + the kwarg's call-site in our orchestrator.

verification.command: source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('backend/agents/orchestrator.py').read()); print('syntax OK')" && ! grep -E 'betas\s*=' backend/agents/orchestrator.py | head -1
verification.live_check: fresh Claude cycle log has zero `unexpected keyword argument 'betas'` errors

steps.name: Breakeven-stop ratchet at +1R (P1.1)
steps.audit_basis: phase-31.0 audit P1.1 (handoff/archive/phase-31.0/experiment_results.md). When position.mfe_pct >= settings.paper_default_stop_loss_pct (1R), mutate stop_loss_price = entry_price. Strict Pareto improvement; zero schema break. Research: Lopez de Prado AFML ch.3; Van Tharp R-multiples; Tradewink ratchet. Carver's anti-breakeven argument acknowledged but breakeven still strictly better than current entry-anchored static stop. Live signal: SNDK/MU/INTC/COHR/WDC/LITE/ON/DELL all crossed +1R and would gain a breakeven floor.

verification.command: python -m pytest backend/tests/test_phase_32_1_breakeven_ratchet.py -v && grep -n '_advance_stop' backend/services/paper_trader.py && python -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('backend/services/paper_trader.py').read())"
verification.live_check: BQ row from financial_reports.paper_positions showing stop_advanced_at_R populated and stop_loss_price = avg_entry_price (NOT entry * 0.92) for at least one current high-MFE position. Run query immediately after deploy.

implementation_plan.summary: When position.mfe_pct >= settings.paper_default_stop_loss_pct (1R = 8% default), advance stop_loss_price from entry-anchored (entry * 0.92) up to entry_price. Idempotent (one-shot per position). Strictly monotonic (never moves down). Pareto improvement over current state. 8 of 11 current positions (SNDK/MU/INTC/COHR/WDC/LITE/ON/DELL) crossed +1R per phase-31.0 baseline and will gain a breakeven floor on first MTM after deploy.
research_topics: Recency-scan delta: anything published 2026-05-20 to now that bears on breakeven-stop ratchet logic. Search: 'breakeven stop systematic trading 2026', 'profit-locking ratchet quant 2026', plus year-less canonical 'breakeven stop trailing'.
Adversarial recheck: any new Carver-style argument against breakeven specifically (Kaminski-Lo Proposition 2 is about TRAILING, not breakeven; confirm no new paper unifies them).

Internal duplicate audit: grep 'rg -n' on backend/ for the symbols breakeven, advance_stop, move_to_breakeven, stop_advance, ratchet. Audit-of-record at signals_server.py:1052-1243 is trailing-stop dead code; confirm NO SEPARATE breakeven helper exists.

Schema migration pattern: read the most recent scripts/migrations/*.py to extract the ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS + idempotent-retry pattern. Confirm bq.save_paper_position's _safe_save_position retry pattern at paper_trader.py:764-773 covers the new field.

MFE unit reconciliation: confirm paper_trader.py:443-446 mfe_pct (max of pnl_pct = (mv - cost_basis)/cost_basis * 100) shares units with settings.paper_default_stop_loss_pct (percent of entry). The audit asserted this; researcher re-verifies.
code_steps: STEP 1: Add helper `_advance_stop(self, pos: dict, new_mfe: float) -> tuple[Optional[float], Optional[str]]` to PaperTrader class in backend/services/paper_trader.py. Place it near other private helpers (e.g., after _update_portfolio_cash at line ~737). Reads pos['avg_entry_price'], pos.get('stop_loss_price'), pos.get('stop_advanced_at_R'). Threshold = settings.paper_default_stop_loss_pct.

STEP 2: Trigger logic: returns (entry_price, now_iso) iff (a) new_mfe >= threshold AND (b) pos.get('stop_advanced_at_R') is None AND (c) stop_loss_price is None OR stop_loss_price < entry_price. Else returns (None, None). Idempotent + monotonic by design.

STEP 3: Wire helper into mark_to_market at backend/services/paper_trader.py:440-456. Place the _advance_stop call AFTER `new_mfe = max(prev_mfe, pnl_pct)` is computed (line 445) and BEFORE `pos.update({...})` (line 449). When (new_stop, advance_iso) is returned non-None, add them to the pos.update dict so they persist in the SAME _safe_save_position call (no race).

STEP 4: Log INFO line on each ratchet fire: 'phase-32.1: ratchet fired for {ticker} -- advanced stop from ${old_stop} to ${entry} at mfe_pct={new_mfe} (threshold ${threshold})'. Use ASCII only per backend-services.md.

STEP 5: Update _POSITION_RT_FIELDS at paper_trader.py:751 to include 'stop_advanced_at_R' so the schema-tolerant retry pattern keeps working on pre-migration BQ tables (defense-in-depth alongside the migration).
migration_steps: STEP 1: Create scripts/migrations/phase_32_1_add_stop_advanced_at_R.py. Idempotent: ALTER TABLE `sunny-might-477607-p8.financial_reports.paper_positions` ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS stop_advanced_at_R STRING.
STEP 2: Match the pattern of the most recent existing migration script (use the same imports, settings load, logger setup).
STEP 4: Verify the column landed via mcp__claude_ai_Google_Cloud_BigQuery__get_table_info on financial_reports.paper_positions. Confirm 'stop_advanced_at_R' appears in fields with type STRING.
STEP 5: Re-run the migration to confirm idempotency (zero error, zero schema change).

test_advance_above_1R: MFE=+20% returns (entry_price, ISO_now). Stop does NOT advance past entry_price (that's 32.2's job).

steps.name: HWM-trailing stop (Chandelier-lite) in live loop + Kaminski-Lo adversarial guard (P1.2)
steps.audit_basis: phase-31.0 audit P1.2. Port signals_server.py:1052-1154 trailing branch into paper_trader. After P1.1, mutate stop_loss_price = max(stop_loss_price, peak_price * (1 - trail_pct/100)). Adversarial guard (Kaminski-Lo Proposition 2): skip trailing when entry_strategy in ('mean_reversion','pairs'). Research: arXiv 2602.11708 (+0.73 Sharpe / 9.7pp DD reduction), Han-Zhou-Zhu 2014 (-49.79% to -11.36% MaxDD), Carver qoppac. Adversarial: Kaminski-Lo 2014 J. Financial Markets Proposition 1+2. LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-05-22 across TWO cycles: LITE (cycle dc3f6cf1) trail-stop fired 16:59 UTC +9.54% pnl 25-day hold; COHR (cycle c7801712) trail-stop fired 18:35 UTC +17.89% pnl 25-day hold capture_ratio=0.63 on MFE 28.36% MAE -6.09%. implementation_plan.summary: After P1.1 (32.1) ships and has at least 1 live cycle, port the trailing-stop logic from signals_server.py:1052-1154 into paper_trader. After breakeven fires, mutate stop_loss_price = max(stop_loss_price, peak_price * (1 - trail_pct/100)) where peak_price = entry_price * (1 + mfe_pct/100). Kaminski-Lo Proposition 2 adversarial guard: SKIP the trailing branch when entry_strategy in {'mean_reversion','pairs'}. MVP: default-on for momentum/triple-barrier; conservative-skip otherwise.
research_topics: Confirm signals_server.py:1052-1154 algorithm is still the correct port target (no newer in-tree alternative)

Recency-scan delta: any new 2026-mid paper on trailing-stop vs static-stop empirical performance
Re-fetch Kaminski-Lo Proposition 2 quote into the contract for the test docstring
code_steps: STEP 1: Extend _advance_stop helper from 32.1 to ALSO compute the trailing branch when stop_advanced_at_R is already set (i.e., breakeven already fired) -- this is the 'after breakeven' condition.
STEP 2: Compute peak_price = pos['avg_entry_price'] * (1 + new_mfe/100). new_trail = peak_price * (1 - settings.paper_trailing_stop_pct/100).
STEP 3: Monotonic max: new_stop = max(current_stop, new_trail). NEVER decrease.
STEP 4: Adversarial guard: load entry_strategy for the position. If in {'mean_reversion','pairs'}, return (None, None) -- DO NOT trail. Else proceed.
STEP 5: New setting paper_trailing_stop_pct (default 8.0). Add to backend/config/settings.py.
STEP 6: Wire into mark_to_market alongside the 32.1 ratchet call.
migration_steps: STEP 1: Add nullable entry_strategy STRING column to paper_positions if lookup path is direct (preferred over join).

STEP 2: Backfill entry_strategy for existing positions from analysis_id -> strategy_decisions (or default to 'momentum' for fail-CLOSED-conservative -- the trail IS applied unless explicitly mean_reversion).
STEP 3: Idempotent ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS.
test_specs: test_trail_advances_on_new_peak: momentum entry, mfe rises 20% to 30% -- stop trails up to peak*(1-8%) = entry*1.30*0.92.
test_trail_monotonic_never_moves_down: peak drops after a new high -- stop stays at the higher level.
test_kaminski_lo_guard_mean_reversion: entry_strategy='mean_reversion', mfe=30% -- stop does NOT trail (stays at breakeven level from 32.1).
test_kaminski_lo_guard_pairs: entry_strategy='pairs', mfe=30% -- stop does NOT trail.
test_default_momentum_trails: entry_strategy='momentum' or None (fail-CLOSED default), mfe=30% -- stop trails.
test_no_regression_full_sweep

Confirm setting paper_trailing_stop_pct does NOT yet exist
hard_guardrails: Trail ONLY fires AFTER breakeven (32.1) has already moved the stop -- chain of operations is breakeven -> trail
Adversarial guard is LOAD-BEARING -- mean_reversion + pairs entries MUST be skipped
Fail-CLOSED-conservative default: when entry_strategy is None/unknown, treat as momentum (trail IS applied). Document in code comment.
stop_loss_price monotonic max ALWAYS
NO take-profit / scale-out logic in this step
Migration idempotent

phases.audit_basis: handoff/current/research_brief_multimarket.md (researcher gate PASSED: 6 sources read in full + recency scan + 9 internal files). KEY FRAMING: this is NOT greenfield -- backend/backtest/markets.py already configures 5 markets (US/NO/CA/EU/KR) with exchange code + currency + timezone + exchange_calendars helper; candidate_selector.get_universe_tickers(market=) takes a market arg but returns [] for non-US (lines 127-132); a phase-5 broker ABC + risk engine exist. THE GAP: the LIVE loop (autonomous_loop.py:310-329) selects the universe via screener.get_sp500_tickers (hard US-only Wikipedia scrape) and never touches markets.py. CORE BLOCKER (50.2): paper_trader.py is currency-BLIND -- quantity*price, cost_basis, nav=cash+positions_value, pnl=mkt-cost (lines 221/243/445-480) with ZERO FX conversion; settings.base_currency/default_market exist but paper_trader never reads them; data_ingestion.py:146 has a literal stub bug ('USD' if market=='US' else 'USD'). SECTOR-BREADTH ANSWER (operator's 'what about health + other sectors?'): NO structural sector exclusion -- the composite score is momentum-weighted (0.4) and US momentum is currently Tech/semi-concentrated, so Health/Industrials/etc are OUT-COMPETED, not excluded; the levers are (a) the EXISTING sector_neutral_momentum_enabled flag (currently OFF) and (b) a broader (international) universe which structurally adds non-Tech sectors (EU = more industrials/financials/healthcare). MISSING DATAPOINTS inventory (the operator's explicit ask): (1) EU+KRX EOD price history [source: yfinance suffixes .DE/.PA/.L/.AS/.KS/.KQ -- FREE but quality-risky; EODHD paid v2]; (2) EU+KRX fundamentals [yfinance partial; EODHD $59.99/mo OWNER-APPROVAL]; (3) FX rates EUR/USD + KRW/USD [yfinance EURUSD=X / KRW=X -- FREE]; (4) market calendars XETR/XLON/XPAR/XKRX [pandas_market_calendars v5.4.0 / exchange_calendars -- FREE, pip-verify]; (5) sector taxonomy reconciliation GICS vs ICB vs local. BIGGEST RISK: yfinance international data quality -- up to 11% deviation vs XETRA + 10-24 days/yr of all-identical OHLC that silently break momentum/RSI/vol signals -> 50.5 adds a data-quality gate. Multi-currency total-return model (arXiv 1611.01463): r = a(r_local - i) + c(r_fx + i). Factor-transfer caveat (PMC8074275): momentum/value replicate in EU+KR but momentum FAILED in Japan -> re-optimize params per market, don't assume US-tuned transfer. 50.1-50.5 are FREE; OWNER-APPROVAL gates: EODHD (paid data, quality v2), pykrx (pip, KR-native). Each step below executes via the FULL harness later (researcher -> contract -> generate -> fresh qa -> log -> flip); they are PLANNED here only.
